thistri: This trigger requires Webflow credentials to access your account.
thesite: Site identifier where the event occurred
selecty: Select your monday.com account for automatic webhook creation.
triggers: Runs on new emails via IMAP (works with any email provider)
imappor: IMAP port (993 for SSL/TLS, 143 for STARTTLS)
imapflow: Search criteria for ImapFlow in JSON object format. Default value: only unread messages.
scopewe: Attach webhook to a specific team
sourcen: Source name if provided via Greenhouse
usedto: Used for creating a webhook in your project. Requires Supporter or Owner role.
gitlabe: Event type from the X-Gitlab-Event header of GitLab
gitref: Branch that was pushed to (e.g., refs/heads/main)
wevalid: We verify requests using this secret key. Pass it in the Authorization header as 'Bearer %s' or in a custom header.
copythi: Copy this code and paste it into your Google Forms Apps Script editor.
thesign: Secret signature from your final station terminal point webhook on the incident.io site. Used for event verification.
actorty: Type of actor (e.g., user, OAuth client, integration)
actorem: E-mail address of the actor (present for user actors in webhook data in Linear)
actorpr: Profile URL of the actor in Linear (different from the top-level entity `url`)
fullcon:
Yes, here is the translation to Russian:

Full content (content: encoded)
asecret: A secret string used for authenticating webhook events. Recommended for increased security. Generate a secure random string (minimum 20 characters recommended).
usersan: Users and bots with access to an entity (`id` + `type` for the object); `type` is either `person` or `bot`. Skipped in some deliveries (considered as empty).
entityi: Entity identifier (page, database, block, comment, or data source identifier)
actorsw: Actors triggering the event (`id` + `type` for the object); `type` can be `human`, `bot`, or `agent` according to Notion.
blocksu: Changes were applied to the blocks as part of the event, if provided by Notion.
thecale: The calendar for monitoring changes in events.
onlytri: Only for specific types of events. By default for all events.
whatyou: What would you like to see.
cdnlink: CDN Media File Link, if provided.
errorid: Error Identifier, null if no error.
e164se: Sender's phone number in E.164 format.
e164re: Recipient/Partner's phone number in E.164 format.
enablet: Option to get full message record data instead of just changes.
theview: View determines what type of content events generate (records, excerpts or histories).
issuecr: Created date for the task (ISO format)
formatg: GitHub Format to be used when sending payload in webhook.
githubv: Sim checks SSL certificates for webhook deliveries.
githube: GitHub event type from the X-GitHub-Event header (e.g., push)
runnumb: Process run number for this workflow
howdate: How dates and times are displayed. Applies only when the Render values function is not available.
ticketp: Priority of the ticket (low, normal, high, urgent)
onlyfir: Only when these columns change. Leave empty for any changes.
whenena: When enabled, each row is returned as an object with a pair of key-value, displayed by column names.
emailac: Email used to send the message
linkeds: Slack channel name associated with this event
shareabl: Meeting link for exchange of information
youratl: Your email account Atlassian login required. An API token is needed for downloading files from the app.
firstap: First user identifier that was ever used.
allapp: All user identifiers that were ever used by the subscriber.
loopwo: Cycle ID (Workflow) present with sourceType equal to
emailob: Email object from payload (ID, emailMessageId, subject)
letthe: Allow the bot to send messages in channels where it is a member.
walkthr: Walkthrough of manifest creation, app installation and insertion of credentials.
buildre: Build result: failed | canceled | partially successful.
emailun: Email/Person who triggered the build or null if not set
userlas: Last update time of the user (data.updated_at)
rawemai: Business Briefing event metadata object for outgoing emails
installa: Installation object containing the integration UUID
whotrig: Who initiated the webhook (user, integrative app or Sentry)
shorthu: A readable short identifier for a problem
issuele: Error level (error, warning and so on.)
fireaft: Light after this number of consecutive failed attempts.
firewhe: If the percent of unreliability exceeds or equals this percentage within the time window, fire a shot.
constant: Fixed identifier for webhooks of automation rules (`gong.automation_rule`). Gong does not send individual event names in the payload.
gongcal: Gong call ID (same as `metaData.id` if present)
enteran: Enter any safe token here. You
firstwh: First WhatsApp Message Package Identifier (wamid) in the Web Hook package text
textbod: Text of the first incoming message packet's main message
emailre: Email timestamp from `data.created_at` useful data, if present, differs from the top-level `created_at`
broadcas: Broadcast ID associated with email, sent as part of a mailing list
tagkey: Metadata keys/values tags attached to the email (payload `data.tags`)
smsstat: SMS Status (e.g., received, sent, delivered, not delivered, failed)
twilioe: Twilio Error Code present in the 'failed' or 'not delivered' status
thesecu: Security Certificate provided by the team when creating outgoing webhook. Used for verifying request authenticity.
fetchho: Hosting content loading and uploading to the store
